nodes.source_location: Kurzfassung / Was mathematisch verifiziert wurde / Go/No-Go

nodes.label: ISO 27001 Technical Readiness and Organization-Level ISMS Gaps

nodes.label: OWASP MASVS Mapping for Android App
nodes.norm_label: owasp masvs mapping for android app

nodes.label: MASVS Level 1 Readiness
nodes.rationale: MASVS-L1 is assessed as fulfilled after storage hardening; WebView bridge and debug separation retain explicit review conditions.

nodes.label: Android WebView JavaScript Bridge Hardening
nodes.rationale: External navigation is moved to the system browser; a manual L2 bridge review remains recommended.

nodes.label: Android Anti-Tampering and Obfuscation Trade-off
nodes.rationale: Obfuscation is intentionally omitted for an open utility because client-side Pro enforcement remains bypassable and extra resilience would add little protection.

nodes.label: Secure Development Lifecycle Controls
nodes.rationale: The assessment maps organizational preparation, software protection, secure production, and vulnerability response to repository evidence.

nodes.label: Automated SAST and SCA Gap
nodes.rationale: Semgrep or CodeQL and pip-audit or osv-scanner are recommended because this review did not add automated SAST or SCA to CI.

nodes.label: Stripe-Hosted Checkout Data Flow
nodes.rationale: Card data is handled by Stripe-hosted Checkout; the application receives only signed webhook metadata and stores no PAN, CVV, or expiry data.

nodes.label: Provisional SAQ A or SAQ A-EP Classification
nodes.rationale: The hosted redirect pattern appears consistent with SAQ A or SAQ A-EP, but only Stripe or the acquirer can determine the binding category.
nodes.norm_label: provisional saq a or saq a-ep classification

nodes.label: Internal Whitebox Security Methodology
nodes.rationale: The review combines static analysis, isolated SQLite reproductions, STRIDE threat modeling, pre-fix reproduction, remediation, and regression retesting without touching production services.

nodes.label: Affiliate Program Internal Penetration Test Results
nodes.norm_label: affiliate program internal penetration test results

nodes.label: AFF-001 Stored XSS in Partner Admin Dashboard
nodes.rationale: Unescaped partner-controlled fields reached admin innerHTML; HTML escaping was added and retested, while unsafe-inline CSP remains a defense-in-depth gap.
nodes.norm_label: aff-001 stored xss in partner admin dashboard

nodes.label: AFF-002 Explicit Intent Referral Attribution Spoofing
nodes.rationale: Android cannot reliably distinguish a verified implicit App Link from an equivalent explicit intent, so the risk is handled through compensating financial controls and anomaly detection.
nodes.norm_label: aff-002 explicit intent referral attribution spoofing

nodes.label: AFF-003 Parallel Webhook Clawback Race Condition
nodes.rationale: Two concurrent webhook deliveries could double-adjust negative balance; checking D1 update changes makes the downstream balance and ledger effects idempotent.
nodes.norm_label: aff-003 parallel webhook clawback race condition

nodes.label: AFF-004 Missing Authentication Rate Limiting
nodes.rationale: D1-backed salted rate limits were added to partner and admin login requests and partner registration.

nodes.label: AFF-005 Mutable GitHub Actions and Wrangler Dependencies
nodes.rationale: Third-party actions use mutable tags and wrangler is downloaded without an exact version in a secret-bearing workflow; verified commit SHAs are required before remediation.
nodes.norm_label: aff-005 mutable github actions and wrangler dependencies

nodes.label: AFF-006 Regex Secret Scan Blind Spot
nodes.rationale: Turnstile patterns were added, but regex-only secret scanning remains bypassable and should be supplemented by a dedicated scanner.
nodes.norm_label: aff-006 regex secret scan blind spot

nodes.label: AFF-007 Dead Dynamic Checkout Implementation
nodes.rationale: An unused checkout implementation could have bypassed feature gating and the 14-day delivery delay; removing it reduced latent attack and maintenance surface.

nodes.label: AFF-008 Case-Sensitive Android Host Comparison
nodes.rationale: Normalizing URI hosts to lowercase prevents legitimate mixed-case App Links from being silently rejected.

nodes.label: AFF-009 Production Android Certificate Fingerprint Verification
nodes.rationale: The correctness of ANDROID_CERT_SHA256 cannot be verified from repository code and must be checked in the production Cloudflare environment.
nodes.norm_label: aff-009 production android certificate fingerprint verification

nodes.label: AFF-010 Unsafe Inline Content Security Policy
nodes.rationale: Allowing unsafe-inline scripts weakens defense against future DOM injection and remains scheduled for frontend hardening.
nodes.norm_label: aff-010 unsafe inline content security policy

nodes.label: AFF-011 Magic-Link Login CSRF
nodes.rationale: An attacker can phish a victim into consuming the attacker's valid magic link, but impact is limited because the dashboard exposes no sensitive victim input fields.

nodes.label: AFF-012 Webhook Stacktrace Disclosure
nodes.rationale: Internal stack details were removed from the webhook response and retained only in server-side logging.

nodes.label: Affiliate Data Minimization
nodes.rationale: Click IP and user agent are salted hashes, buyer PII is not exposed to affiliate UIs, and Android stores only a public referral slug plus timestamp.

nodes.label: Expired Affiliate Data Retention Gap
nodes.rationale: Expired clicks, tokens, and sessions remain physically stored indefinitely because no automated cleanup job exists.

nodes.label: Partner Data Subject Rights Process Gap
nodes.rationale: No technical partner access or deletion workflow is implemented; the organizational process remains to be defined.
nodes.norm_label: partner data subject rights process gap

nodes.label: Production Go-Live Security Gates
nodes.rationale: Production activation requires SHA-pinned CI, verified Android certificate configuration, Stripe scenario tests, backup restore proof, PCI and legal review, alerting, cleanup automation, CSP hardening, and written owner approval.

nodes.label: Automatic Production No-Go Conditions
nodes.rationale: Active payout freeze, failed integrity controls, open critical or high findings, unverified backups or webhooks, missing secrets, unclear PCI scope, missing responsible owners, or no rollback automatically block release.

nodes.label: A1 License Key Forgery Hypothesis
nodes.rationale: The hypothesis was disproved because 96-bit random keys are created and validated server-side with no embedded signing secret or invertible payload.

nodes.label: A2 Local Pro Enforcement Bypass
nodes.rationale: A device owner can patch cached license state or exploit offline grace; this is accepted as proportionate for low-cost on-device software rather than addressed with server-side DRM.

nodes.label: A3 Scrape Endpoint SSRF
nodes.rationale: Authenticated scrape requests could reach internal addresses; scheme and resolved-IP allowlisting plus per-redirect revalidation now block private, loopback, link-local, reserved, and multicast targets.

nodes.label: A4 Local Sensitive File Permissions
nodes.rationale: World-readable local state, password, and license files were hardened to directories mode 0700 and files mode 0600.

nodes.label: A5 Desktop Auto-Login Secret Exposure
nodes.rationale: The long-lived web password was replaced in the URL by a single-use 120-second token exchanged through a dedicated login endpoint.

nodes.label: A6 Loopback Login Throttle
nodes.rationale: Brute-force weakness was disproved because constant-time password comparison and a five-failure, 60-second per-IP lockout were already present.

nodes.label: A7 Core Web Security Headers
nodes.rationale: CSP, frame denial, MIME sniffing protection, and no-referrer policy were added centrally to all server responses.

nodes.label: A8 Path Traversal Hypothesis
nodes.rationale: The hypothesis was disproved because downloads resolve only DB-recorded basenames and static content enforces resolved-path containment.

nodes.label: A9 yt-dlp Self-Update Supply Chain
nodes.rationale: The updater uses TLS, SHA-256 verification, downgrade prevention, and archive traversal checks, so the tested attack hypothesis was disproved.

nodes.label: A10 Stripe Webhook Forgery Hypothesis
nodes.rationale: The hypothesis was disproved by HMAC-SHA256 verification, constant-time comparison, and a five-minute replay window.

nodes.label: CORE-001 Client-Side Pro Enforcement Residual Risk
nodes.rationale: Server-side DRM would violate the product's offline and on-device promise and is disproportionate to a 12-euro purchase; reevaluate only if abuse becomes measurable or subscriptions are introduced.
nodes.norm_label: core-001 client-side pro enforcement residual risk

nodes.label: CORE-002 Local Data and Loopback HTTP Residual Risk
nodes.rationale: TLS on loopback and local database encryption are not justified for the supported single-user model; hardened permissions and optional secure cookies are compensating controls.
nodes.norm_label: core-002 local data and loopback http residual risk

nodes.label: Canonical Finding Status: 6 Fixed, 1 Partial, 5 Residual
nodes.rationale: The register is the authoritative count: six findings fixed and retested, one partially fixed, five residual or structural, with no open P0 or P1 findings.
nodes.norm_label: canonical finding status: 6 fixed, 1 partial, 5 residual

nodes.label: Affiliate Financial Integrity Invariants
nodes.rationale: Payouts are bounded by assigned licenses and qualified commissions, allocated to individual commissions, protected against duplicates and races, and frozen on integrity or reconciliation failures.

nodes.label: SOC 2 Security and Processing Integrity Readiness
nodes.rationale: Security and processing integrity controls are assessed as ready, including role separation, rate limiting, maker-checker payout authorization, reconciliation, and fail-closed integrity gates.
nodes.norm_label: soc 2 security and processing integrity readiness

nodes.label: SOC 2 Availability, Confidentiality, and Privacy Gaps
nodes.rationale: Availability cannot be assessed without production access, while alerting, retention automation, data-subject processes, an observation period, and independent CPA review remain outstanding.
nodes.norm_label: soc 2 availability, confidentiality, and privacy gaps

nodes.label: Partner and Admin Session Trust Boundary
nodes.rationale: Server-side role checks separate partner and admin sessions; violating this boundary would be a critical privilege escalation.
nodes.norm_label: partner and admin session trust boundary

nodes.label: Cloudflare to Stripe Trust Boundary
nodes.rationale: Inbound trust uses signed webhooks and outbound trust uses a secret API key; live Stripe state remains the financial source of truth.

nodes.label: Android App and Website Attribution Boundary
nodes.rationale: The app accepts two exact hosts and a claim path, while the website applies the same server-side attribution logic to app and browser traffic.
nodes.norm_label: android app and website attribution boundary

nodes.label: Affiliate Single Points of Failure
nodes.rationale: D1 is the only persistence layer, Stripe availability gates payouts, Resend gates every login, and one admin email has no fallback.
